executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 103 organizations publicly listed as victims in the past 48 hours, representing a coordinated mass campaign. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (hit by both CoinbaseCartel and ShinyHunters), Markham Stouffville Hospital, National Money Mart (database leaked), and Peterborough Public Health. The Qilin ransomware group leads the assault with 20 victims, followed by SafePay (14), Play (11), and a massive Clop campaign (15 victims). Critical infrastructure spanning healthcare, education, financial services, and municipal government has been compromised, signaling a national-scale cyber emergency requiring immediate coordinated response.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 20 Canadian organizations including Eastern Townships School Board, Workers Health & Safety Centre, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), Waterloo Information Systems, Kuzco Lighting, Service Star Freightways, Canada Goose, Traffic Tech, Watermark Beach Resort, Questica, CST Coal, and multiple accounting/law firms
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1078 (Valid Accounts), double extortion tactics with public victim shaming

items.name: SafePay
items.targets: 14 Canadian organizations including Teccart college (education), transportation carriers (briwaycarriers.com), construction firms (groupepiche.ca, kenalex.ca, silverlinegroupinc.com), and manufacturing (precisionaluminum.ca)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter)

items.name: Play
items.targets: 11 Canadian organizations including Equine Canada, Chemical Computing Group, Paisley Products, Makivik, Security ONE Alarm Systems, ALLMAX Nutrition, and Pewarchuk CPA
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1003 (OS Credential Dumping), known for targeting managed service providers and supply chains

items.name: Clop
items.targets: 15 Canadian organizations including law firms (SMTLAWYERS.CA), technology providers (CLOUDDATAWORKS.CA, SENSIBLESOLUTIONS.CA, TECHNIKEL.CA), hospitality (WHISKIJACKRESORTS.COM), manufacturing (IDEALWELDERS.COM), and helicopter services (CMHHELI.COM)
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), historically exploits file transfer vulnerabilities (MOVEit, GoAnywhere)

items.name: DragonForce
items.targets: 6 Canadian organizations including phoenixlabs.com, Esposito Bros. Construction, McKee-Pownall Equine Services, Emond Publishing, CCR Solutions, and SOTECK
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter), T1047 (Windows Management Instrumentation)

items.name: TheGentlemen
items.targets: 4 Canadian organizations including Labtician Ophthalmics (healthcare), ControlGMC (manufacturing), Groupe Caddac, and Real Tech
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1566 (Phishing), T1078 (Valid Accounts)

items.name: Lynx
items.targets: 5 Canadian organizations including Peterborough Public Health, KTL Group, Laurysen Kitchens, Group Echo, and Trucash
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1562 (Impair Defenses), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery)

items.name: Sinobi
items.targets: 4 Canadian organizations including Kids & Company (childcare services), Active Green + Ross (automotive), Morison Insurance Brokers (financial services), and The Sundher Group

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces a national-scale ransomware emergency with 103 confirmed victims across all sectors in 48 hours. Critical infrastructure compromised includes two healthcare facilities (Markham Stouffville Hospital, Labtician Ophthalmics, McKee-Pownall Equine, Thunder Bay Counselling, Bison Family Medical), education institutions (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart college, College Superieur De Montreal), municipal government (Municipality of North Perth), and public health (Peterborough Public Health, Workers Health & Safety Centre). No CCCS advisories were published in this reporting period despite the scale of attacks.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose was hit by two separate ransomware groups (CoinbaseCartel and ShinyHunters) in a coordinated attack. Active Green + Ross (automotive retail) and Kids & Company (childcare services retail) also compromised.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered confirmed database exfiltration by Everest ransomware group. Morison Insurance Brokers hit by Sinobi. Multiple accounting firms compromised: Pewarchuk CPA (Play), MG Chartered Professional Accountant (Qilin), and law firm SMT Lawyers (Clop).

report_2w.executive_summary: The Canadian government issued an official alert for CVE-2026-3055, a vulnerability in Citrix NetScaler ADC and Gateway—organizations using these products must prioritize patching immediately. Two major supply chain attacks are unfolding: North Korean threat actors (UNC1069) compromised the widely-used Axios npm package to distribute remote access trojans, and a campaign dubbed TeamPCP breached the European Commission and approximately 1,000 SaaS environments by poisoning the Trivy security scanning tool. Critical vulnerabilities in Cisco IMC (CVE-2026-20093) allow unauthenticated admin access to UCS servers, and Progress ShareFile flaws enable unauthenticated remote code execution. Iranian-linked Handala group breached Israeli defense contractor PSK Wind Technologies, and North Korean hackers stole $285 million from Drift cryptocurrency platform in 10 seconds.

items.name: UNC1069 (North Korea-linked)
items.targets: Axios npm package maintainer via social engineering; supply chain attack affecting downstream users globally
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise: Software Supply Chain), T1566 (Phishing), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter) - deployed remote access trojan via compromised npm packages

items.name: TeamPCP (ShinyHunters-linked)
items.targets: European Commission AWS environment (30 EU entities affected), Trivy security scanner supply chain, Sportradar, ~1,000 SaaS environments
items.ttps: T1195.001 (Supply Chain Compromise: Software), T1199 (Trusted Relationship), T1078 (Valid Accounts) - tag poisoning and credential theft via compromised CI/CD scanning tool

items.name: Handala (Pro-Iran)
items.targets: Israeli defense contractor PSK Wind Technologies, Stryker (March cyberattack)
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1566 (Phishing) - geopolitically motivated attacks targeting defense sector

items.name: Lazarus Group (North Korea)
items.targets: Drift cryptocurrency platform ($285 million theft in 10 seconds), GitHub-based C2 infrastructure
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), T1071.001 (Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols), T1102 (Web Service) - LNK-based phishing with GitHub as C2, cryptocurrency theft

items.name: Kimsuky (North Korea)
items.targets: Not specified in articles
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment), T1204.002 (User Execution: Malicious File) - malicious LNK files deploying Python backdoors

items.name: Qilin (Ransomware)
items.targets: Faulkner County Sheriff's Office
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery) - ransomware operations

items.name: Akira (Ransomware)
items.targets: Woodland Trade, American Vintage Home, Briggs Plumbing Products, Genco Manufacturing
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery) - ransomware operations targeting manufacturing and retail

items.name: Phorpiex Botnet operators
items.targets: Widespread botnet activity
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1657 (Financial Theft), T1566 (Phishing) - distributing ransomware, sextortion campaigns, cryptocurrency clipping malware

items.name: Venom Stealer operators
items.targets: ClickFix social engineering victims
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1204.002 (User Execution: Malicious File), T1560 (Archive Collected Data) - ClickFix lures leading to data exfiltration

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL26-006 on March 30, 2026, warning organizations about CVE-2026-3055 affecting Citrix NetScaler ADC and Gateway—this is an official government advisory requiring immediate attention. No direct breaches of major Canadian retailers (Loblaws, Canadian Tire, SportChek, Shoppers Drug Mart) or financial institutions (CIBC, RBC, TD, Scotiabank, BMO) were identified in this reporting period. Canadian organizations should prioritize patching Citrix NetScaler, Cisco IMC, and Progress ShareFile vulnerabilities, and review npm dependencies for Axios package compromise.
